-- 4 of 7 --

In this lesson I’m going to walk you through a very
important concept that you need to know if you’re going
to scale your Facebook Ads. This concept is “Delayed
Attribution.”
I’ll also walk you through my Delayed Attribution
Calculator Spreadsheet so that you can make use of
this right away.
Why is this important? You need to know what
channel/ad is driving the revenue so that you can
reinvest MORE of that revenue back into the ads that are
working, and make better decisions.
What is Attribution and Delayed Attribution?
� Attribution:
The Data uncovered that shows the customer’s path to
conversion (purchase).
The Model is the lens you choose to view the data with
and associate a value to each channel.
For Facebook, a conversion is recorded if it falls within
two parameters:
• 	If user viewed Ad and Converted within 1 Day.
• 	If user clicked Ad and Converted within 28 Days.
� Delayed Attribution:
Credit appended to specific advertising Channel/Ad +
time that has past.
*As we increase the amount of marketing channels we
use to acquire customers, we will look to build out more
detailed Attribution Models to accurately assign a cost to
the respected channels used to acquire a customer.
LESSON:
FOUNDATIONS (PART B):
DELAYED ATTRIBUTION
MODULE:
SCALING WITH
FACEBOOK ADS
